schema_diagram.description: Entity-Relationship diagram showing database schema and relationships
schema_diagram.content:
erDiagram
    USERS ||--o{ POSTHISTORY : "has"
    POSTS ||--o{ POSTHISTORY : "has"
    USERS ||--o{ COMMENTS : "has"
    POSTS ||--o{ COMMENTS : "has"
    POSTS ||--o{ POSTLINKS : "has"
    POSTS ||--o{ POSTLINKS : "has"
    POSTS ||--o{ TAGS : "has"
    USERS ||--o{ VOTES : "has"
    POSTS ||--o{ VOTES : "has"
    POSTS ||--o{ POSTS : "has"
    USERS ||--o{ POSTS : "has"
    USERS ||--o{ POSTS : "has"
    USERS ||--o{ BADGES : "has"

    BADGES {
        int Id PK
        int UserId FK
        text Name
        datetime Date
    }
    POSTHISTORY {
        int Id PK
        int PostHistoryTypeId
        int PostId FK
        text RevisionGUID
        datetime CreationDate
        int UserId FK
        text Text
        text Comment
        text UserDisplayName
    }
    COMMENTS {
        int Id PK
        int PostId FK
        int Score
        text Text
        datetime CreationDate
        int UserId FK
        text UserDisplayName
    }
    USERS {
        int Id PK
        int Reputation
        datetime CreationDate
        text DisplayName
        datetime LastAccessDate
        text WebsiteUrl
        text Location
        text AboutMe
        int Views
        int UpVotes
        int DownVotes
        int AccountId
        int Age
        text ProfileImageUrl
    }
    POSTLINKS {
        int Id PK
        datetime CreationDate
        int PostId FK
        int RelatedPostId FK
        int LinkTypeId
    }
    TAGS {
        int Id PK
        text TagName
        int Count
        int ExcerptPostId FK
        int WikiPostId
    }
    VOTES {
        int Id PK
        int PostId FK
        int VoteTypeId
        datetime CreationDate
        int UserId FK
        int BountyAmount
    }
    POSTS {
        int Id PK
        int PostTypeId
        int AcceptedAnswerId
        datetime CreaionDate
        int Score
        int ViewCount
        text Body
        int OwnerUserId FK
        datetime LasActivityDate
        text Title
        text Tags
        int AnswerCount
        int CommentCount
        int FavoriteCount
        int LastEditorUserId FK
        datetime LastEditDate
        datetime CommunityOwnedDate
        int ParentId FK
        datetime ClosedDate
        text OwnerDisplayName
        text LastEditorDisplayName
    }

badges.name: badges
badges.description: The badges table stores records of user-earned badges, capturing recognition or achievements within the system.

comments.name: comments
comments.description: The comments table stores user-submitted feedback with associated scores, primarily reflecting neutral or low-scoring interactions based on the distribution where 77.4% of entries have a score of 0.

examples: 'This question appears to be off-topic because it is has no statistical content other than finding a function/specific R package. You can try http:/www.rseek.org '
'This question appears to be only about data minapulation in R and thus belongs on StackOverflow (where it's been asked numerous times already). '
'This question appears to be off-topic because it is solely about software. It's not suitable for Stack Overflow either. '
'This question appears to be off-topic because it is about Stata. It should probably be asked on a list devoted to Stata. '
'This question appears to be off-topic because it is entirely about which software syntax is appropriate. See the help for more advice if unclear. '
'John '

postHistory.name: postHistory
postHistory.description: The postHistory table tracks the complete revision history of posts, including initial creations, edits, deletions, undeletions, migrations, and other moderation actions, with support for both user-identified and anonymous/system-generated changes.

PostHistoryTypeId_values.content: 2=Initial Body(30.34%), 5=Edit Body(22.90%), 3=Initial Title(14.16%), 1=Initial Title(14.16%), 4=Edit Title(5.28%), 6=Rollback Body(4.20%), 25=Post Undeleted(3.40%), 24=Post Deleted(2.32%), 16=Community Owned(0.72%), 10=Post Locked(0.59%), 36=Post Migrated In(0.49%), 33=Post Reopened(0.40%), 34=Post Merged Away(0.34%), 13=Post Unprotected(0.15%), 12=Post Protected(0.15%)

business_rules.content: When UserId is NULL but UserDisplayName is present, it represents an anonymous or system-generated edit (e.g., community wiki or migration events)

examples: 'Contingency table (chi-square). Also Logistic Regression is your friend - use dummy variables. '

'I'm looking for worked out solutions using Bayesian and/or logit analysis similar to a workbook or an annal. \\n\\nThe worked out problems could be of any field; however, I'm interested in urban planning / transportation related fields. '
'What algorithms are used in modern and good-quality random number generators? '
'[Weka][1] for data mining - contains many classification and clustering algorithms in Java.\\n\\n [1]: http://www.cs.waikato.ac.nz/ml/weka  '

'I wouldn't really call R "windows based" :) That's like saying the cmd prompt is windows based. \\n\\nRapidMiner is far better [1]. It's a free, open-source, multi-platform, GUI. Here's a video on time series forecasting:\\n\\nhttp://rapidminerresources.com/index.php?page=financial-time-series-modelling---part-1\\n\\nAlso, don't forget to read:\\n\\nhttp://www.forecastingprinciples.com/\\n\\n\\n[1] No, I don't work for them. '

quality_issues.description: Contains 24482 empty string values in addition to NULLs

examples: 'fixed punctuation '
'Edited out the dis-incentive to pay for physical book. '
'Fixed due to Baltimark suggestion. '
'tried to make a short intuitive answer first and then develop... easier to read ? '
'added more info '

postLinks.name: postLinks
postLinks.description: The postLinks table captures relationships between posts, primarily indicating linked references (91.7%) and duplicate posts (8.3%).

LinkTypeId_meaning.content: 1 indicates a linked post (reference), 3 indicates a duplicate post (the RelatedPostId is a duplicate of PostId)

posts.name: posts
posts.description: The posts table stores user-generated content including questions, answers, and wiki entries from a Q&A platform, with the majority being answers (52.6%) and questions (47.4%).

examples: 'Model selection is a problem of judging which model from some set performs best. Popular methods include $R^2$, AIC and BIC criteria, test sets, and cross-validation. To some extent, feature selection is a subproblem of model selection. '
'Forecasting involves estimating the value or distribution of a random variable which has not yet been observed. '
'SPSS (Statistical Package for the Social Sciences) is a proprietary cross-platform general-purpose statistical software package. '
'Mathematica is a software package for symbolic mathematical computations. '
'The probability of receiving a treatment given a set of observed covariates. '
'Robust t-test for mean '
'Difference between Excel's RAND(), RAND()*RAND(), etc '
'Classical measurement error model in R '
'How do I fit a multilevel model for over-dispersed poisson outcomes? '
'What are the software limitations in all possible subsets selection in regression? '
'Natasha '
'Kip '
'Agnese Marino '
'Mahmoud Ghandi '
'Sriram '

tags.name: tags
tags.description: The tags table stores metadata labels used to categorize or annotate other entities within the system.

users.name: users
users.description: The users table stores core information about registered individuals in the system, including their identities, contact details, and account metadata.

examples: 'adolf garlic '
'Sarah  '
'Tormod '
'Mithun P '
'Alos '
'C# ASP.NET developer for a couple of years. Posessed with an active programming spirit and with a never-ending desire to learn more. '

'MSc Student / Assistant Researcher
\\\\nData Mining & Quantitative Finance '
'Quantitative ecologist using Bayesian methods to build models that improve decision making for environmental management.  '
'I'm a graduate student in computational biology working in the lab of Dr. David Baker. I develop code for Rosetta@Home, and I am interested in using computers to learn about the world. '

' - I love Algorithms, Programming, AI and machine learning\\\\n
\\\\n - In my spare time, I like playing real time strategy games, chess, watching movies.'

votes.name: votes
votes.description: The votes table records user interactions on content, primarily capturing upvotes (89.5%), favorites (9.0%), and accepted answers (3.9%), along with rarer moderation actions like downvotes, spam flags, and post closures.
